methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
General Matter, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Scott Nolan and Lee Robinson, has raised $50M+ to date. The sector comp universe includes 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers spanning advanced reactor development and fuel cycles, including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Stage-progression analysis, the sole applicable triangulation method, uses the $50M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression to subsequent funding rounds, yielding a $100M implied valuation. Peer-median analysis was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables in the nuclear energy sector—while 8 nuclear peers exist, fewer than 3 met the narrow-tier filters required for statistical reliability, and a broader cross-sector median would lack relevance.

The triangulated valuation range is $85M–$115M, with a median of $100M. This range reflects the single-method constraint and carries material uncertainty regarding appropriate stage-based multipliers for early-stage nuclear ventures, which trade at heterogeneous risk and timeline profiles. The company's specific technology and competitive positioning within advanced reactors remain key value drivers not captured by stage-based mechanics alone. Additional funding rounds and peer benchmarking will refine this estimate.
